title: DuckDB hangs when COPY command processes NUL (chr(0)) if quote=''
description:
### What happens?

A NUL (chr(0)) in a string seems to cause DuckDB considerable grief.

(1) At the CLI

select 'a�b';

simply hangs, even though `select 'a' || chr(0) || 'b';` produces:
a\0b

(2) More seriously, the `copy` command can also cause DuckDB to hang:
```
create or replace table t as (from values ('a' || chr(0) || 'b') t(i));
copy t to 'csv2tsv.tsv' (header false, delimiter '\t', escape '', quote '');
```
Note that this particular problem seems to be triggered by specifying `quote` to be the
empty string.


### To Reproduce

```
create or replace table t as (from values ('a' || chr(0) || 'b') t(i));
copy t to 'csv2tsv.tsv' (header false, delimiter '\t', escape '', quote '');
```

### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.3-dev158

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Peter Koppstein

### Affiliation:

Princeton University

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

key_bug_features: COPY command hangs with NUL character and empty quote
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, value BLOB);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'test' || chr(0), NULL);
CREATE TABLE another_table AS SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL;
COPY test_table TO 'output.tsv' (header true, delimiter '	', escape '', quote '');
SELECT * FROM another_table WHERE name LIKE '%' || chr(0) || '%';
